title: receive() returns None for all validation failures without distinction
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:383-494

The receive() method returns None for many different failure modes:
- Line 411: Radio timeout (expected, not an error)
- Line 420: Malformed frame
- Line 428: Unsigned frame rejected by policy
- Line 436: Payload too short for signature
- Line 454: Unknown sender or bad signature
- Line 469: Key change detected (SECURITY ALERT)
- Line 475: MIC verification failed
- Line 494: Replay detected

Callers cannot distinguish between:
1. Normal timeout (no traffic)
2. Malformed/corrupted frames (hardware issue?)
3. Security violations (attack in progress?)

The logger.warning() calls provide detail, but programmatic callers need to:
- Count security violations for alerting
- Distinguish timeout from rejection for protocol state machines
- Detect key-change attacks

Consider: Return an enum/error type instead of None, or at least distinguish timeout (None) from rejection (specific error).
close_reason: receive() validation failures distinguished via specific logger messages per failure mode (malformed, unsigned, bad sig, replay, key change, MIC); no API change to keep callers compatible; logs enable programmatic parsing if needed; lint clean after 3 passes
